sections.heading: 1. Introduction
content: These Terms & Conditions ("Terms") constitute a legally binding agreement between you ("Client," "User," or "You") and Adymire Agency ("Company," "We," "Our," or "Us") governing your use of our website, services, and products.
By accessing our website, submitting any form, engaging our services, or entering into any agreement with us, you acknowledge that you have read, understood, and agreed to be bound by these Terms.
If you do not agree to these Terms, you must not use our website or services.

sections.heading: 2. Company information
content: Adymire Agency is a software and technology services company specializing in:

sections.heading: 3. Definitions
content: For the purposes of these Terms:
"Company" refers to Adymire Agency and its subsidiaries, divisions, and affiliated entities.
"User" or "Client" refers to any individual, company, or organization that accesses our website or uses our services.
"Services" refers to all offerings provided by the Company including but not limited to design, development, marketing, automation, and consulting services.
"Project" refers to any specific work engagement or deliverable agreed upon between the Company and the Client.
"Agreement" refers to any contract, proposal, statement of work, or legally binding arrangement between the parties.
"Website" refers to our official website and any associated platforms or applications.
"Deliverables" refers to any outputs, products, or results produced by the Company for the Client.
"Intellectual Property" refers to copyrights, trademarks, patents, trade secrets, and other proprietary rights.

sections.heading: 4. Acceptance of terms
content: By using our website or services, you automatically accept these Terms along with all other applicable policies including:
These documents collectively form the complete agreement between you and the Company.
Users must explicitly agree to these Terms by:
• Checking the acceptance box on website forms
• Continuing to use services after being notified of updates

subSections.heading: 5.1 Service offerings
content: The Company provides the following categories of services:

subSections.heading: 5.2 Service customization
content: All services are customized based on client requirements, project scope, and mutual agreements. The Company reserves the right to:
bullets: Determine the feasibility of requested services
Set timelines based on project complexity
Define scope boundaries and limitations
Recommend alternative solutions when appropriate

sections.heading: 6. User obligations
content: As a user of our services, you agree to:

subSections.heading: 6.1 Provide accurate information
bullets: Submit truthful and complete information in all forms and communications
Update information promptly if changes occur
Provide valid contact details for project communication

subSections.heading: 6.2 Cooperate in project execution
bullets: Respond to requests for information, feedback, or approvals within reasonable timeframes
Provide necessary assets, content, or resources as agreed
Designate authorized representatives for decision-making
Attend scheduled meetings and calls

subSections.heading: 6.3 Make timely payments
bullets: Pay invoices according to agreed payment terms
Notify the Company immediately of any payment issues
Maintain valid payment methods on file

subSections.heading: 6.4 Respect intellectual property
bullets: Not claim ownership of Company's proprietary methods, tools, or processes
Not reverse-engineer, copy, or reproduce Company deliverables without authorization
Not share confidential information with third parties

subSections.heading: 6.5 Comply with laws
bullets: Use services only for lawful purposes
Not request services that violate laws, regulations, or ethical standards
Ensure your business and content comply with applicable laws

subSections.heading: 7.1 Company reserves the right to:
bullets: Accept or decline any project or client at its discretion
Modify project scope, timelines, or pricing based on changing requirements
Subcontract or delegate work to qualified third parties
Terminate services for policy violations or non-payment
Update these Terms and policies at any time
Suspend or discontinue certain service offerings
Use completed projects in portfolio and marketing materials (with client consent)

subSections.heading: 7.2 Company is responsible for:
bullets: Delivering services in a professional and timely manner
Maintaining confidentiality of client information
Providing clear communication and project updates
Honoring agreed-upon timelines and deliverables (subject to client cooperation)
Ensuring quality of work meets industry standards

subSections.heading: 8.1 Pricing structure
bullets: Pricing is determined based on project scope, complexity, timeline, and resources required.
All prices are quoted in the agreed currency (typically INR or USD).
Prices are subject to change based on scope modifications.
The Company's pricing decisions are final and non-negotiable unless mutually agreed otherwise.

subSections.heading: 8.2 Payment terms
content: (Payment milestones may vary based on project size and agreement)
• Monthly or annual billing in advance
• Auto-renewal unless cancelled before billing date
• Billed at the end of each month
• Payment due within 7-14 days of invoice

subSections.heading: 8.3 Invoicing
bullets: Invoices will be sent via email to the designated billing contact
Invoices include applicable taxes (GST, VAT, etc.)
Late payment may incur interest charges or service suspension
Payment gateway fees and transaction charges are borne by the client

subSections.heading: 8.5 Late payment consequences
bullets: Grace period: 7 days from due date
After grace period: Project suspension
After 30 days: Project cancellation and legal action
Late fees: Up to 2% per month on overdue amounts

subSections.heading: 9.1 Scope definition
bullets: Project scope will be clearly defined in the agreement or proposal
Scope includes specific deliverables, timelines, and boundaries
The Company controls final decisions on scope interpretation

subSections.heading: 9.2 Change requests
bullets: Any changes to the agreed scope must be requested in writing
Change requests will be evaluated for feasibility and impact
Additional charges will apply for scope expansions
Timeline extensions may be necessary for significant changes
The Company reserves the right to decline change requests that significantly alter the project

subSections.heading: 9.3 Revisions
content: Number of revisions will be specified in the agreement
Revisions must be based on the original scope
Additional revisions beyond agreed limits will incur extra charges

subSections.heading: 10.1 Project timelines
content: • Timelines are estimates based on the information available at project start
• Actual delivery may vary due to:
- Client delays in providing feedback or assets

subSections.heading: 10.2 Client responsibilities for timeline
content: Timely project completion depends on client:
bullets: Providing required information and assets on time
Responding to queries and approval requests promptly
Being available for scheduled meetings and reviews
Minimizing scope changes during execution

subSections.heading: 10.3 Delivery process
bullets: Deliverables will be provided through agreed channels (email, cloud storage, project management tools)
Client must review and provide feedback within the specified timeframe
Final delivery occurs upon client acceptance or after the review period expires

subSections.heading: 11.1 Client-provided materials
bullets: Client retains ownership of all materials provided to the Company (logos, content, images, etc.).
Client grants the Company a license to use these materials for project execution.
Client warrants that they have the right to use all provided materials.

subSections.heading: 11.2 Company-created deliverables
content: Ownership of deliverables depends on the agreement:
• Upon full payment, client receives ownership of final deliverables
• Company retains ownership of underlying code, frameworks, tools, and methodologies
• Company may reuse generic components, templates, or code libraries in other projects
• If explicitly agreed and paid for, client receives full ownership including source materials
• Premium charges apply for complete intellectual property transfer

subSections.heading: 11.3 Company proprietary assets
content: The Company retains full ownership of:
bullets: Proprietary tools, platforms, and software
Development methodologies and processes
Templates, frameworks, and code libraries
Internal documentation and systems

subSections.heading: 11.4 Portfolio and marketing rights
bullets: The Company may showcase completed projects in its portfolio
The Company may use project details in case studies and marketing materials
Client logos and names may be displayed on the Company website
Clients may request to be excluded from public portfolio

subSections.heading: 12.1 Company obligations
bullets: Maintain confidentiality of client business information
Not disclose proprietary information to third parties
Implement reasonable security measures to protect client data
Limit access to client information to authorized personnel only

subSections.heading: 12.2 Client obligations
bullets: Maintain confidentiality of Company's proprietary methods and tools
Not share access credentials or sensitive project materials
Not disclose pricing or confidential agreements

subSections.heading: 12.3 Exceptions
content: Confidentiality obligations do not apply to:
bullets: Information already in the public domain
Information independently developed by either party
Information required to be disclosed by law or court order

subSections.heading: 13.1 Company warranties
bullets: Services will be performed with reasonable skill and care
Deliverables will substantially conform to agreed specifications
Company has the right to provide the contracted services

subSections.heading: 13.2 Disclaimer of warranties
content: TO THE MAXIMUM EXTENT PERMITTED BY LAW:
bullets: Services are provided "AS IS" without warranties of any kind
The Company does not guarantee specific business results or outcomes
The Company is not responsible for third-party tools, platforms, or services
The Company does not warrant that services will be uninterrupted or error-free

subSections.heading: 14.1 Direct damages
content: The Company's total liability for any claims arising from services will not exceed the total amount paid by the Client for the specific project or service.

subSections.heading: 14.2 Indirect damages
content: TO THE MAXIMUM EXTENT PERMITTED BY LAW, THE COMPANY SHALL NOT BE LIABLE FOR:
bullets: Loss of profits, revenue, or business opportunities
Loss of data or information
Business interruption or downtime
Consequential, indirect, or special damages
Damages arising from third-party actions or tools

subSections.heading: 14.3 Exceptions
content: Limitation of liability does not apply to:
bullets: Gross negligence or willful misconduct by the Company
Breach of confidentiality obligations
Intellectual property infringement by the Company
Damages that cannot be limited by law

subSections.heading: 15.1 Client indemnification
content: The Client agrees to indemnify and hold harmless the Company from any claims, damages, or expenses arising from:
bullets: Client's use of deliverables or services
Content, materials, or instructions provided by the Client
Violation of third-party rights by Client-provided materials
Client's breach of these Terms or applicable laws

subSections.heading: 15.2 Company indemnification
content: The Company agrees to indemnify the Client from claims that Company-created deliverables infringe on third-party intellectual property rights, provided:
• Client promptly notifies the Company of the claim
• Client provides reasonable cooperation in defense
• Company has sole control of defense and settlement

subSections.heading: 16.1 Use of third-party tools
content: Services may depend on or integrate with third-party platforms including:
bullets: Hosting providers (AWS, Google Cloud, etc.)
Content delivery networks
Payment gateways
Advertising platforms (Google Ads, Facebook Ads)
Analytics tools
API services
AI and automation platforms

subSections.heading: 16.2 Third-party limitations
content: The Company is not responsible for:
bullets: Third-party service outages or disruptions
Changes to third-party APIs or pricing
Third-party terms of service or policies
Data handling by third-party services
subSections.note: Clients are responsible for obtaining necessary accounts and accepting third-party terms.

sections.heading: 17. Data protection and privacy
bullets: Collection and use of personal data is governed by our Privacy Policy.
Clients consent to data processing as described in the Privacy Policy.
The Company implements reasonable security measures to protect data.
Clients are responsible for complying with data protection laws for their own users.

sections.heading: 18. Force majeure
content: Neither party shall be liable for failure to perform obligations due to events beyond reasonable control, including:
bullets: Natural disasters (earthquakes, floods, pandemics)
War, terrorism, civil unrest
Government actions, strikes, or labor disputes
Internet or utility failures
Acts of God
sections.note: Affected party must notify the other party promptly and make reasonable efforts to resume performance.

subSections.heading: 19.1 Termination by company
content: The Company may terminate services immediately if:
bullets: Client breaches these Terms or other policies
Client fails to make payments
Client engages in illegal or unethical conduct
Continuation of service is not feasible

subSections.heading: 19.2 Termination by client
bullets: Clients may request termination subject to the Cancellation Policy
Final decision on termination requests rests with the Company

subSections.heading: 19.3 Effects of termination
bullets: Client must pay for all work completed
Company may retain ownership of incomplete deliverables
Both parties remain bound by confidentiality obligations
Intellectual property provisions remain in effect

subSections.heading: 20.1 Informal resolution
content: Parties agree to first attempt to resolve disputes through:
• Good faith discussions to reach a mutual resolution

subSections.heading: 20.2 Mediation
content: If informal resolution fails, parties may agree to:

subSections.heading: 20.3 Arbitration / legal proceedings
content: • Disputes will be resolved through arbitration or court proceedings
• Jurisdiction and governing law as specified in Section 21

subSections.heading: 20.4 Legal fees
content: The prevailing party in any legal dispute may be entitled to recover reasonable legal fees and costs.

subSections.heading: 21.1 Governing law
content: These Terms are governed by the laws of India and the state/region where the Company is registered.

subSections.heading: 21.2 Jurisdiction
content: Any legal proceedings shall be brought exclusively in the courts of [Your City/State], India.

subSections.heading: 21.3 International clients
content: • Governing law remains as specified above
• Clients consent to jurisdiction of Indian courts
• Alternative dispute resolution may be negotiated

subSections.heading: 22.1 Right to modify
content: The Company reserves the right to update, modify, or replace these Terms at any time for reasons including:

subSections.heading: 22.2 Notification
bullets: Updated Terms will be posted on our website with a new "Last Updated" date
Significant changes may be communicated via email
Continued use of services constitutes acceptance of updated Terms

subSections.heading: 22.3 Client rights
bullets: Clients who do not agree with updated Terms may discontinue services
Existing signed agreements remain governed by the terms in effect at signing unless otherwise specified

sections.heading: 23. Severability
content: If any provision of these Terms is found to be invalid, illegal, or unenforceable:
• The remaining provisions shall continue in full force and effect
• The invalid provision shall be modified to achieve the intended purpose to the maximum extent possible

sections.heading: 24. Entire agreement
content: These Terms, together with all referenced policies and any signed agreements, constitute the entire agreement between the parties and supersede all prior:
No modification is valid unless made in writing and signed by both parties.

sections.heading: 25. Waiver
bullets: Failure to enforce any provision does not constitute a waiver
Waiver of any breach does not waive subsequent breaches
All rights and remedies are cumulative

sections.heading: 26. Assignment
bullets: Clients may not assign or transfer rights or obligations without Company consent
The Company may assign these Terms to affiliates or successors
Assignment does not affect confidentiality or payment obligations

sections.heading: 27. Notices
content: All notices must be sent in writing to:
FOR COMPANY: Adymire Agency, Email: [Your Official Email], Address: [Your Business Address]
FOR CLIENT: To the email address or contact information provided in the service agreement.
• 5 business days if sent via postal mail

sections.heading: 28. Contact information
content: For questions about these Terms & Conditions, please contact us:

sections.heading: 29. Acknowledgment and acceptance
content: By using our website, submitting forms, or engaging our services, you acknowledge that you have:
• Read and understood these Terms & Conditions in their entirety
• Agreed to be legally bound by these Terms
• Authority to enter into this agreement on behalf of your organization (if applicable)
IF YOU DO NOT AGREE TO THESE TERMS, YOU MUST NOT USE OUR SERVICES.
